channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Rising returns, but Utes fall flat against ASU, 27-19
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (ABC4 Sports) - Cam Rising was back at quarterback, but the Utes offense continued to struggle to score touchdowns in a 27-19 loss at Arizona State Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cam Skattebo runs for 156 yards and 2 TDs, Arizona State knocks off No. 16 Utah 27-19
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Kenny Dillingham screamed through an adrenaline-fueled interview and got caught up in a student mosh pit. The sea of humanity enveloping him, the Arizona State coach and alum joined in, jumping and shouting with the students after Arizona State's 27-19 victory over No.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Scores: Week 8 of the 2024 high school football season
item.description: Every week, high school athletes across the region prove that big time players make big time plays in big time games. (Like the Los Angeles Dodgers did in the deciding Game 5 of the NLDS against the San Diego Padres on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rick Pitino makes surprise visit to Kentucky, says 'I'm so happy to be back' to support Mark Pope
item.description: LEXINGTON, Ky. (AP) — Rick Pitino was wildly cheered in a surprise return to Rupp Arena, with the Hall of Fame coach saying “I am so happy to be back” in a show of support for first-year Kentucky coach and former captain Mark Pope on Friday night at the Big Blue Madness public practice.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All aboard! Mets and Yankees only 2 stops away from first Subway Series in 24 years
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two stops away from a Subway Series. When the New York Yankees advanced to the American League Championship Series on Thursday night, that put both Big Apple teams in baseball's final four for the first time in 24 years.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Yamamoto outduels Darvish in historic matchup as Dodgers beat Padres 2-0 to reach NLCS
item.description: Yoshinobu Yamamoto outdueled Yu Darvish in a historic playoff matchup of Japanese-born starters, and the Los Angeles Dodgers got home runs from Kiké Hernández and Teoscar Hernández to beat the San Diego Padres 2-0 and advance to the National League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harrison Butker endorses Donald Trump: 'The most pro-life president'
item.description: In an appearance on "The Ingraham Angle" Thursday night, Kansas City Chiefs kicker Harrison Butker announced his support for Donald Trump in this year's election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor Swift fans side with Caitlin Clark after Angel Reese interviews Travis Kelce's ex-girlfriend
item.description: Taylor Swift fans are flocking to support Caitlin Clark, a lifelong Chiefs fan, after Angel Reese interviewed the ex-girlfriend of Swift's boyfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WNBA has just 1 Black head coach but more could come from a pool of strong assistants in the league
item.description: The WNBA has been praised in the past as a leader for its diversity hiring practices. That hasn't changed, but some things have. At the end of the 2022 season, half of the 12 head coaches in the league were Black.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NFL concussion protocol was followed when Bills QB Josh Allen was evaluated in Houston, league says
item.description: The NFL and its players association said Friday the required steps were followed when Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen was evaluated for a concussion last week at Houston and cleared to return to the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tigers ace Skubal gets start as Detroit faces Cleveland in Game 5 winner-gets-Yankees ALDS matchup
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Tarik Skubal's itching to have another emotional October outburst that makes Tigers fans roar — even if it embarrasses his mom.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bay Area high school football league addresses viral “BTA” TikTok trend causing fights across nation
item.description: The Oakland Athletic League takes proactive measures to prevent postgame brawls caused by viral social media trend.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: A.J. Foyt still drawing big crowds on or off the track in Indianapolis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — As the sun sets on a perfect, late summer evening, A.J. Foyt sits inside his wine vault in Speedway, Indiana. He's getting regular updates on the expanding line that snakes around tables, through the bar, out the front door, down Main Street and around the corner for two blocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers will start Yamamoto against San Diego's Darvish in winner-take-all Game 5 of NLDS
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani will get his wish to see Los Angeles Dodgers teammate Yoshinobu Yamamoto face off against San Diego's Yu Darvish in Game 5 of the National League Division Series.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Be careful when buying Dodgers-Padres NLDS Game 5 tickets online, USAA says
item.description: Friday night will bring the biggest baseball game in Los Angeles in years as the San Diego Padres visit the Dodgers for a winner-take-all Game 5 in the National League Division Series. And the high stakes are bringing high ticket prices.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Djokovic to meet Fritz in Shanghai semis and Sabalenka to play Gauff in Wuhan
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — Novak Djokovic and Taylor Fritz advanced to a semifinal matchup at the Shanghai Masters on Friday. Four-time champion Djokovic fought back from a set down to beat Czech player Jakub Mensik 6-7 (4), 6-1, 6-4 in the quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Forest fined almost $1M for questioning integrity of match officials in inflammatory post
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An inflammatory post that got more than 46 million views on social media has proven very costly for Premier League soccer team Nottingham Forest.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL great believes panic in Jets’ organization played a role in Robert Saleh’s firing
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Famer and Green Bay Packers legend LeRoy Butler says panic in the New York Jets organization led to the firing of head coach Robert Saleh.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coach K discusses 'chaotic' state of college athletics as NIL runs rampant: 'Figure this out'
item.description: Mike Krzyzewski retired in 2022 and has seen NIL run rampant, but despite his decades in college sports, even he doesn't have a solution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL officials' missed call during 49ers-Seahawks game bewilders fans
item.description: NFL officials puzzled fans and players alike on Thursday night over a missed call during a game between the Seattle Seahawks and San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah HC wins overtime thriller over Islanders, 5-4
item.description: LONG ISLAND, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - In its first ever road game, the Utah Hockey Club proved to be extremely resilient. Dylan Guenther scored his second goal at 2:18 of overtime, and the Utah Hockey Club rallied for a 5-4 win against the New York Islanders at UBS Arena on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Giancarlo Stanton hits go-ahead homer in the eighth, Yankees beat Royals 3-2 in Game 3 of the ALDS
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Giancarlo Stanton hit a go-ahead homer in the eighth inning amid a battle of bullpens, and the New York Yankees beat the Kansas City Royals 3-2 on Wednesday night in Game 3 of their AL Division Series at Kauffman Stadium.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: BYU looks to pick up where it left off after bye week
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It's not ideal to have a bye week while on a five-game winning streak, but #14 BYU needed the rest after getting off to a 5-0 start.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fruita's Olivia Wirth gets family support on senior night
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Growing up the youngest of three in a competitive, athletic household, Fruita Monument senior Olivia Wirth learned the value of hard work from her parents and siblings.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Mets take 2-1 series lead over rival Phillies
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six months of success for the Philadelphia Phillies could be wiped out before the leaves start to turn. With one more loss to the New York Mets, a 95-win regular season would become an afterthought.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Dodgers pitcher reportedly robbed of luxury watch at Southern California racetrack
item.description: Dodgers pitcher Walker Buehler was reportedly robbed of a luxury watch at a horse racing track in what appeared to be a grand theft spree perpetrated by multiple organized groups.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Chicago Fire FC names Gregg Berhalter, former U.S. Men's National Team coach, as director of football and head coach
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago Fire FC has hired former U.S. Men's National Soccer Team head coach Gregg Berhalter as its head coach and director of football, the Major League Soccer team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Another state regulator targets Brewers owner over alleged sand theft in Malibu
item.description: A second state agency is targeting the owner of the Milwaukee Brewers for taking sand from a Malibu beach.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball fans discuss Dodgers’ chances in NLDS Game 2 over donuts
item.description: After a rollercoaster of emotions in Game 1 of the National League Divisional Series, baseball lovers were up early getting donuts in Inglewood and eager to share their thoughts with the news on Sunday's matchup between the Los Angeles Dodgers and the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Bears vs. Panthers: What to know for Sunday's Week 5 matchup at Soldier Field
item.description: Carolina (1-3) at Chicago (2-2) Sunday, Noon, Fox UPDATE: The Bears announced their list of inactives, via their X feed. They are WR Velus Jones Jr., FB Khari Blasingame, DL Zacch Pickens, DB Terrell Smith and DL Dominique Robinson.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
